Cash Flow

M
Finance AI · Cash Flow
High confidenceOrders + ledger + providersLast sync: today 06:14
Profit ≠ cash
You booked $240K profit but generated $176K cash
The $64K gap is gateway holds and inventory you paid for upfront. Profitable on paper, tighter in the bank — worth watching as you scale.
Runway floor
Lowest point is $312K on day 47
A supplier batch and the quarterly tax payment land the same week. You stay well above zero, but that's the tightest the next 90 days get.
Conversion cycle
Cash cycle lengthened to 34 days
Up from 28 last quarter — inventory is sitting longer before it sells. Each added day ties up roughly $5K in working capital.
Profit vs cash — what you earned vs what actually landed

actually generated↑ $19K vs last quarter

Free cash flowGateway holdsInventory
Profit is an accounting story; cash is what you can spend. The slice to the right of the line is profit you “earned” but can’t touch yet — which is how a profitable store still misses payroll.

Next 90 days — where the cash line goes

at day 90
Today+30 days+60 days+90 days
You dip to $312K on day 47 — a supplier batch and the quarterly tax payment land together — then recover to $1.02M by day 90. The floor holds; no action needed, but that week is the one to watch.
